behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is the lint rule logic (pattern/AST detection) on template text. No real I/O or model behavior is required, and mocks would not hide the core detection bug at this boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the lint function multiple template strings: one with verbatim/near-verbatim phase instructions from SKILL.md, one clean control, and one borderline paraphrase. Assert reject/accept decisions and explicit failure reason codes so regressions in leakage detection fail immediately.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is leakage introduced during template interpolation/fixture wiring, which a unit test on a static template can miss. Needs real harness assembly path and fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual prompt-building pipeline with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, capture the final assembled prompt string, and run leakage checks on that final artifact. Fail if any phase-specific instruction text (or normalized equivalent) appears.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module wiring plus filesystem-loaded skill content. In-process mocks can mask accidental hard-coding, so the test must exercise real loader + real files together.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the same test entrypoint used in CI with a temporary plugin/skill directory. Assert the constructed prompt/context includes content loaded from SKILL.md paths and does not include hard-coded fallback phase rules embedded in test code.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: This is a true causality check that behavior quality depends on real skill context. Correctness depends on real model responses, which are non-deterministic; mocks would likely hide this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run A/B executions against the same user-style prompt: baseline skill content vs. intentionally degraded/removed target section. Call the real model, keep all else constant, and assert the structured success metric drops below threshold (or failure rate rises) for degraded skill across repeated trials.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bug occurs across components (model call wrapper, artifact writer, parser). Unit tests on parser alone miss whether capture happens first in the real pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the inference pipeline with a deliberately malformed/non-JSON response. Assert a raw-output artifact is persisted with timestamp/run-id, then assert parser failure occurs afterward. Fail if artifact is missing or written only post-parse.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often comes from interaction between extraction/parsing and assertion layers: echoed keywords can pass weak checks. Need end-to-end assertion against parsed structure, not isolated keyword matcher only.
behaviors.test_description: Use two realistic outputs through the same extraction+assertion path: (a) prompt-echo text containing expected keywords but wrong schema/field placement, (b) correctly structured output. Assert (a) fails and (b) passes based on schema/field constraints.
